files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 49c3660..ed9a005 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,52 +1,48 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 35 actions (Day 245)
-- **Cash after actions: $3,699,749.** Week35 net **+$1.262M**: subscription cash **$5.038M** vs costs **$3.775M** (ops $2.237M, compute $1.014M, cap $196k, dev $184k, ads $140k). Active book **167,740 individual subs + 4,096 active enterprise seats** (dashboard may show 4,115); active gross MRR **~$19.13M/mo**.
-- Active MRR core: S2 B **$13.84M**, S2 A **$3.32M**, D_S07 B **$1.27M**, S2 C **$317k**, D_S07 C/A **$193k**, E3 **$163k**. Delivered quality remains very strong after t8: S2 B **7.76**, D_S07 B **5.97**, E3 B **6.10**. **Do not upgrade B to tier4** unless retention collapses; B4 roughly doubles compute.
-- Week35 acquisition: **4,750 new individual subs**, **2,541 cancellations**, **2,813 upgrades**, no outage/overload. S2 content ads at $20k/day produced **2,423 paid S2 subs** (1,287 B / 1,130 A / 6 C) and ~**$296k first-cycle cash** on $140k ad spend; scaled to **$25k/day** because support is clean and first-cycle ROI is still >2x gross.
+## Current strategy after Week 36 actions (Day 252)
+- **Cash after actions: $2,740,730** (was $4,074,063 before starting R&D). Week36 net before actions/R&D was only **+$374k**: subscription cash **$4.112M** vs costs **$3.738M** (ops $2.132M, compute $1.046M, cap $196k, dev $184k, ads $175k). Active book **169,876 individual subs + 4,096 enterprise seats**; active gross MRR **~$19.43M/mo small + $0.163M/mo enterprise**.
+- Core book: S2 B **98,156 subs / $14.23M MRR**, S2 A **46,972 / $3.18M**, D_S07 B **21,018 / $1.25M**, S2 C **$375k**, D_S07 C/A **$214k**, E3 **$163k**. Delivered quality remains high (Day252 S2 B **7.96**, D_S07 B **6.04**, E3 B **6.17**) but competitor posts on Days251-252 announced large quality jumps (**~0.475 + ~0.399**) and S2 social now has citation/support complaints. **Do not upgrade B to tier4** unless retention collapses; B4 roughly doubles compute.
+- **R&D:** Started repeat **tier8 t8_2 on Day252**, cost **$1.333M**, excellent sample: **due Day294, +0.7257 Q**. Existing t9 still due **Day375, +0.2201**. Do **not** start t10 until cash is clearly rebuilt (target **>$7-8M**) and D_S07 ops taper/ad efficiency are stable; t10 cost $1.667M and likely affects 12wk cash before payoff.
 
-## Day245 final config in force
-- Core: **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 5M / C 15M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**.
+## Day252 final config in force
+- Core: **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 10M / C 50M** (raised B/C to remove enterprise cap friction), capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**.
 - **Promotions:** standing full map preserved. Group: D_S07 **$60**, D_S04 **$70**, S3 **$40**, S1 **$60**, D_S03/05/06 **$70**, D_S10 **$50**, S2/enterprise **$0**. Group-plan: D_S04 B/C **$20**, D_S07 B **$40** (D_S07 B effective target **$59**). Lead promos: S2/D_S07 **$10**; D_S04/S1/D_S03/05/06/10 **$20**; S3 **$50**; enterprise **$0**.
-- **By-customer one-cycle S2 first-renewal promos for due days 246-252:** **4,340 customers** = S2 A **2,010 @ $30**, S2 B **2,315 @ $40**, S2 C **15 @ $60**. IMPORTANT Day252: replace this by_customer map for next due cohort; **query per plan or paginate** because a single query can silently truncate at 5,000 rows.
-- Ads: **content_marketing -> S2 $25k/day** (full replacement). Watch support and market-cap saturation; if support stays clean this is likely cash-positive, but cut back to $20k/$15k if issues age or cash weakens.
-- Ops targeted full replacement: **S2 $245k**, **D_S07 $45k** (tapered from $60k), E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04/E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$299.6k targeted + $5k global = $304.6k/day**.
-- Dev targeted: **S2 $12k**, **D_S07 $2k**, E3 $2k, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 $1k each, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 $0.05k each = **$21.25k targeted + $5k global = $26.25k/day**.
-- Estimated fixed daily burn after actions: ops/dev/ads/capacity **~$383.85k/day**; compute recently **~$145k/day**; lead cost ~$0.8k/day => **~$530k/day** before subscription cash. Next 7d current-active gross due (before churn/plan changes/promos/new subs) **$3.85M**.
-
-## Retention / cohort notes
-- Post-t8 first-renewal B retention is excellent: cohort_start 210 current S2 B **3,406/3,480 active (97.9%)**, D_S07 B **610/613 active (99.5%)**; cohort_start 203 S2 B **97.0%**, D_S07 B **87.0%**. Caveat: cohort_start includes some not-yet-due and upgrades into B.
-- S2 A as A remains weak (cohort_start 210 A only **194/2,595 active as A**), but many A customers upgrade into B; keep A/B/C first-renewal by_customer discounts **30/40/60** for now.
-- D_S07 is likely low/negative margin at high support: last week D_S07 compute **~$216k/w**, MRR run-rate **~$1.47M/mo**, and ops at $60k/day was too high. Day245 D_S07 ops cut to **$45k/day**; restore only if active issues age or S2 reputation deteriorates.
-
-## Support / reputation observations
-- Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: S2 **3,401**, D_S07 **611**, D_S10 **24**, tiny others. Week35 opened/resolved: S2 **24,295 / 24,091** (slight backlog but day0), D_S07 **4,828 / 5,034** (clearing after prior taper).
-- All-issues table still has stale cancelled-customer tickets: S2 **11,793 avg 49.7d**, D_S07 **8,382 avg 34.8d**; social may still mention old multi-day tickets despite active queue being same-day.
-- Social improved: posts 698-701 on Day245 are positive on support/quality/document synthesis; one negative S2 support-silence post 697. Agent post **id36 Day245** replied to 697: named owner/tracking ID/ETA/>24h escalation/same-day active queues. Monitor replies/effects. Avoid broad regulated-energy/health claims that can trigger enterprise caution.
+- **By-customer one-cycle S2 first-renewal promos for due days 253-259:** **4,032 customers** = S2 A **1,864 @ $30**, S2 B **2,153 @ $40**, S2 C **15 @ $60**. IMPORTANT Day259: replace this by_customer map for next due cohort; **query per plan or paginate** because a single query can silently truncate at 5,000 rows.
+- Ads: **content_marketing -> S2 $20k/day** (cut from $25k/day because $25k produced fewer paid subs and social/support pressure rose). Compare ROI: Day245-252 at $25k/day got **2,310 paid active S2 subs / ~$283k first-cycle cash** on $175k spend; previous $20k weeks were more efficient (~2.4k paid subs on $140k spend).
+- Ops targeted full replacement: **S2 $255k**, **D_S07 $30k** (taper from $45k), E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04/E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$294.6k targeted + $5k global = $299.6k/day**. If D_S07 or S2 active ticket ages exceed day0/1, restore D_S07 to $45k and/or raise S2.
+- Dev targeted: **S2 $25k** (citation/legal accuracy sprint), D_S07 $2k, **E3 $3k**, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 $1k each, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 $0.05k each = **$35.25k targeted + $5k global = $40.25k/day**.
+- Estimated fixed daily burn after actions: ops/dev/ads/capacity **~$387.85k/day**; compute recently **~$149k/day**; lead cost ~$0.65k/day => **~$538k/day** before subscription cash. Next 7d current-active gross due (before churn/plan changes/promos/new subs) **$3.193M** so Day259 cash may be flat/slightly down despite R&D already paid.
+
+## Retention / acquisition notes
+- Week36 acquisition/cancellations: **4,478 new individual subs**, **2,342 cancels**, **2,424 upgrades**, no outage/overload. Paid S2 content at $25k/day: 2,310 active (1,237 B / 1,066 A / 7 C) + 425 lost; spend/lead worsened to **$64**.
+- S2 active first-renewal cohorts around created days 223-245 show ~82-86% active before first billing; older created 210-222 now mostly active as B/C after renewal (daily active_any among all created leads ~46-59% because denominator includes lost leads). Keep A/B/C first-renewal by_customer discounts **30/40/60** for now.
+- D_S07 remains likely low/negative margin at high support: weekly D_S07 compute **~$222k**, MRR run-rate **~$1.46M/mo (~$341k/w)**, so ops must stay low. Day252 D_S07 ops cut to **$30k/day**; restore quickly if active issues age or D_S07/S2 social worsens.
+
+## Support / social observations
+- Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: S2 **2,970**, D_S07 **628**, D_S10 **26**, tiny others. Week36 opened/resolved by resolved-day: S2 **22,691 opened / 22,786 resolved**, D_S07 **4,376 / 4,349**, avg resolution **1 day**. All-issues table still has stale cancelled-customer tickets: S2 **11,698 avg 55d**, D_S07 **8,409 avg 41d**.
+- Social Day252 had fresh negatives: post 727 S2 support handoffs/two days lost; 726/725 S2 citation/tax accuracy drift; 720-722 competitor quality shocks. Agent post **id37 Day252** replied to post 726: citation-accuracy gate, source-link checks, regression tests, named-owner updates, publish pass-rate/backlog-age deltas. Monitor replies/effects. Avoid broad regulated-energy/health claims.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day245 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent = 0**.
-- Day245 responded to **2,069 new enterprise leads / 1,190,391 seats**. Active enterprise remains **3 E3 customers / 4,096 seats / $163.2k MRR**; no new enterprise seats in Week35. Pipeline has many latest-agent lead threads; response times/close rates are long and low.
-- Current enterprise offer bases/floors: **E1 A$5/B$11/C$22; E2 A$18/B$45/C$89; E3 A$22/B$39/C$69; D_E02 A$10/B$24/C$49; D_E07 A$26/B$49/C$89; D_E08 A$18/B$40/C$75; D_E09 A$6/B$16/C$32.** Volume discounts 0.95 >=1k seats, 0.90 >=3k seats but not below floors. Keep unit economics; do not chase huge unprofitable seat blocks.
+- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day252 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent = 0**.
+- Day252 responded to **1,871 new enterprise leads / 1,063,585 seats**. Active enterprise remains **3 E3 customers / 4,096 seats / $163.2k MRR**; no new enterprise seats in Week36. Pipeline is very fat but historical close rate is low/slow.
+- Current enterprise offer bases/floors after Day252: **E1 A$5/B$11/C$22; E2 A$18/B$45/C$89; E3 A$20/B$35/C$62; D_E02 A$10/B$24/C$49; D_E07 A$26/B$49/C$89; D_E08 A$18/B$40/C$75; D_E09 A$6/B$16/C$32.** 0.95 volume discount for >=1k seats but not below floors (E3 floors A18/B32/C58 etc.). Keep unit economics; do not chase huge unprofitable seat blocks.
 - If many seats accept, immediately reassess compute/capacity. Scale to **tier7** if active enterprise seats exceed ~250k or peak usage approaches >65M/day; add enterprise ops/dev.
 
-## R&D / research
-- Completed: t1 +0.0289 Day86; t2 +0.0619 Day93; t3 +0.1023 Day100; t4 +0.1078 Day107; t5 +0.0840 Day114; t6_1 +0.5559 Day140; t6_2 +0.4556 Day170; t7 +0.001 Day190; t4_2 +0.1018 Day191; **t8 +0.6261 Day231**.
-- In progress: **t9 due Day375 +0.2201** (bad/late). No new R&D before cash is clearly **>$8M** and the $25k/day ads + D_S07 ops taper are stable; then consider t10 (cost $1.667M, mean 127d, mean +0.85Q) if competitor/retention pressure warrants it.
-
-## Day252 priorities
-1. Run report: cash/ledger, active MRR, due billings, compute by group, active+all issue ages, social replies/effects for id36, exact first-renewal cohorts 210-222, enterprise accepts/counters, capacity/usage.
+## Day259 priorities
+1. Run report: cash/ledger, active MRR, due billings, compute by group, active+all issue ages, social replies/effects for id37, first-renewal cohorts, enterprise accepts/counters, capacity/usage.
 2. **Reset S2 by_customer promos** for next first-renewal cohort. Query per plan/paginate to avoid 5,

import novamind_api as nm
for start,end in [(224,231),(231,238),(238,245),(245,252)]:
    print(start,end,nm.query(f"SELECT SUM(leads_generated) leads,SUM(spend) spend FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>{start} AND day<={end}")['rows'])
    print(nm.query(f"""
    SELECT c.acquisition_source,c.group_id,s.status,s.plan,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN s.status IN ('active','subscribed') THEN s.effective_price ELSE 0 END),2) mrr
    FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
    WHERE c.created_day>{start} AND c.created_day<={end} AND c.acquisition_source='content_marketing'
    GROUP BY c.acquisition_source,c.group_id,s.status,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC
    """)['rows'][:10])

import novamind_api as nm
q=lambda s:nm.query(s)['rows']
rows=q('''
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)m
 ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.sender, latest.thread_type, COUNT(*) n, SUM(latest.seat_count) seats, ROUND(AVG(latest.seat_count),1) avg_seats,
       MAX(latest.seat_count) max_seats, SUM(CASE WHEN latest.seat_count>=3000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n3000,
       SUM(CASE WHEN latest.seat_count>=3000 THEN latest.seat_count ELSE 0 END) seats3000
FROM latest JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND latest.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id, latest.sender, latest.thread_type ORDER BY seats DESC''')
print('non-agent open')
for r in rows: print(r)
print('agent pipeline by group')
for r in q('''
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)m
 ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid)
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(latest.seat_count) seats, ROUND(AVG(latest.seat_count),1) avg_seats, MAX(latest.seat_count) max_seats,
       SUM(CASE WHEN latest.seat_count>=3000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n3000,
       SUM(CASE WHEN latest.seat_count>=3000 THEN latest.seat_count ELSE 0 END) seats3000
FROM latest JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND latest.sender='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC'''):
    print(r)

import novamind_api as nm
print('day', nm.vars.current_day)
print('cash', nm.query('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger')['rows'])
print('t8', [t for t in nm.research.list_research_projects()['tiers'] if t['tier']==8])
print('open_nonagent', nm.query('''WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid) SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(latest.seat_count) seats FROM latest JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE latest.closed=0 AND latest.sender!='agent' AND c.customer_type='large' ''')['rows'])

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
from collections import Counter
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Week36 actions day', cur)

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY_ERROR', e, sql[:500]); return []
active = "s.status IN ('active','subscribed')"

# Core packaging: preserve S2 cash engine; raise B/C quotas to remove enterprise-cap friction without changing normal small-user compute.
print('prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print('tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4))
print('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=10000000, C=50000000))
print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
try:
    print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
except Exception as e:
    print('ads_strength_error', e)

# Content ads remain first-cycle profitable but efficiency fell at $25k/day and social/support pressure rose; step back to proven $20k/day.
print('ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 20000.0}}))
print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000.0, development=5000.0))

# Replace one-cycle S2 first-renewal customer promos for next 7 billing days. Query per plan to avoid the 5k-row truncation trap.
by_customer = {}
counts = Counter()
for plan, disc in [('A',30.0), ('B',40.0), ('C',60.0)]:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan,
           ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until,
           c.created_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE {active}
      AND c.customer_type='small'
      AND c.group_id='S2'
      AND s.plan='{plan}'
      AND c.created_day BETWEEN {cur-36} AND {cur-7}
      AND ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) BETWEEN 1 AND 7
    ORDER BY s.customer_id
    """)
    print('s2_first_renewal_rows', plan, len(rows))
    for r in rows:
        by_customer[str(int(r['customer_id']))] = disc
        counts[(r['group_id'], r['plan'])] += 1
print('s2_first_renewal_customer_promos', len(by_customer), dict(counts))

by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0,
    "D_S07": 60.0,
    "D_S04": 70.0,
    "S3": 40.0,
    "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0,
    "D_S05": 70.0,
    "D_S06": 70.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0,
    "E2": 0.0,
    "E3": 0.0,
    "D_E02": 0.0,
    "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0,
    "D_E09": 0.0,
}
by_group_plan = {
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 40.0},
}
promo_res = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan)
print('promotion_summary', {'global': promo_res.get('global'), 'n_customer_promos': len(promo_res.get('by_customer', {})), 'groups': promo_res.get('by_group'), 'by_group_plan': promo_res.get('by_group_plan')})

lead_by_group = {
    "S2": 10.0, "D_S07": 10.0,
    "D_S04": 20.0, "S3": 50.0, "S1": 20.0,
    "D_S03": 20.0, "D_S05": 20.0, "D_S06": 20.0, "D_S10": 20.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0, "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0,
}
print('lead_promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_by_group, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# Ops: invest a bit more in S2 ownership while tapering low-margin D_S07, whose active tickets remained day0 at $45k/day.
ops = {
    "S2": 255000.0,
    "D_S07": 30000.0,
    "E3": 5000.0,
    "S3": 1000.0,
    "D_S04": 500.0,
    "E2": 500.0,
    "D_E02": 500.0,
    "D_E07": 500.0,
    "D_E08": 500.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S1": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 100.0,
    "D_S06": 100.0,
    "D_S10": 100.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
ops_res = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops)
print('targeted_ops', {'total_extra_per_day': ops_res.get('total_extra_per_day'), 'targeted_spend': ops_res.get('targeted_spend')})

# Dev: competitor launches created a large expectation jump. Put incremental spend into S2 citation/legal accuracy and a little into E3 enterprise readiness.
dev = {
    "S2": 25000.0,
    "D_S07": 2000.0,
    "E3": 3000.0,
    "D_E02": 1000.0,
    "E2": 1000.0,
    "D_E07": 1000.0,
    "D_E08": 1000.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S3": 250.0,
    "D_S04": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 50.0,
    "D_S06": 50.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "S1": 50.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
dev_res = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev)
print('targeted_dev', {'total_extra_per_day': dev_res.get('total_extra_per_day'), 'targeted_spend': dev_res.get('targeted_spend')})

# R&D: after two competitor quality shocks, start a repeat t8 (sooner/cheaper than t10) if not already in progress. Keep enough cash buffer.
try:
    projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
    t8 = next((t for t in projects.get('tiers', []) if t.get('tier') == 8), {})
    cash = q('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')[0]['cash']
    print('pre_rd_cash', cash, 't8_status', t8)
    if (t8.get('in_progress') or 0) == 0 and cash and float(cash) > 2500000:
        print('start_research_t8', nm.research.start_research_project(tier=8))
    else:
        print('skip_research_t8')
except Exception as e:
    print('research_start_error', e)

# Social: address the current citation-accuracy complaint with concrete gates and metrics, not broad regulated-industry claims.
try:
    content = "David, citation drift is unacceptable. This week we’re shipping a citation-accuracy gate: source-link checks, regression tests from reported misses, and named-owner updates on each ticket. We’ll publish pass-rate/backlog-age deltas before asking teams to trust it again."
    print('social_len', len(content))
    print('social', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=726))
except Exception as e:
    print('social_error', e)

# Enterprise: answer every latest non-agent open thread (including system new leads) with contribution-positive offers.
q_threads = f"""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
  JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
    ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT latest.customer_id, c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.turn_number,
       latest.seat_count AS turn_seats, latest.offer_json, latest.message_text,
       s.status AS sub_status, s.seat_count AS sub_seats
FROM latest
JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND latest.sender!='agent' AND c.customer_type='large'
ORDER BY latest.day ASC, latest.customer_id ASC
"""
threads = q(q_threads)
print('enterprise_threads_to_answer', len(threads), 'turn_seats', sum((r.get('turn_seats') or 0) for r in threads), 'sub_seats', sum((r.get('sub_seats') or 0) for r in threads))
for k,v in Counter((r['group_id'],r['thread_type'],r['sender'],r['turn_number'],r['sub_status']) for r in threads).most_common(30):
    print('enterprise_awaiting_summary', k, v)

# Slightly sharper E3/enterprise quota posture, but keep E2/D_E02/E1/D_E09 above compute+capacity risk.
base = {
    'E1':   [['A', 5.0],  ['B', 11.0], ['C', 22.0]],
    'E2':   [['A', 18.0], ['B', 45.0], ['C', 89.0]],
    'E3':   [['A', 20.0], ['B', 35.0], ['C', 62.0]],
    'D_E02':[['A', 10.0], ['B', 24.0], ['C', 49.0]],
    'D_E07':[['A', 26.0], ['B', 49.0], ['C', 89.0]],
    'D_E08':[['A', 18.0], ['B', 40.0], ['C', 75.0]],
    'D_E09':[['A', 6.0],  ['B', 16.0], ['C', 32.0]],
}
floors = {
    'E1':   {'A':4.0, 'B':10.0, 'C':20.0},
    'E2':   {'A':16.0,'B':43.0, 'C':82.0},
    'E3':   {'A':18.0,'B':32.0, 'C':58.0},
    'D_E02':{'A':8.0, 'B':20.0, 'C':41.0},
    'D_E07':{'A':22.0,'B':40.0, 'C':80.0},
    'D_E08':{'A':15.0,'B':32.0, 'C':65.0},
    'D_E09':{'A':6.0, 'B':16.0, 'C':31.0},
}

def offers_for(r):
    g = r.get('group_id')
    offers = [x[:] for x in base.get(g, [['A',12.0], ['B',30.0], ['C',60.0]])]
    seats = r.get('turn_seats') or r.get('sub_seats') or 0
    # Modest volume discount for 1k+ seats, but do not undercut floors. Max new lead <2k seats this week.
    disc = 0.95 if seats >= 1000 else 1.0
    fl = floors.get(g, {})
    offers = [[p, round(max(price*disc, fl.get(p, 0.0)), 2)] for p, price in offers]
    # If counteroffers appear, meet B/C only when still above floor and sane relative to cost.
    try:
        oj = json.loads(r.get('offer_json') or '{}')
        vals = []
        if isinstance(oj, dict):
            for k in ('price','target_price','counter_price','price_per_seat'):
                if k in oj: vals.append(float(oj[k]))
            for k in ('offers','counter_offers'):
                if isinstance(oj.get(k), list):
                    for o in oj[k]:
                        if isinstance(o, dict):
                            for kk in ('price','price_per_seat','counter_price'):
                                if kk in o: vals.append(float(o[kk]))
        if vals:
            p = min(vals)
            if p >= fl.get('B', 10.0):
                offers.insert(0, ['B', round(p, 2)])
            if p*1.45 >= fl.get('C', 25.0):
                offers.insert(1, ['C', round(max(p*1.45, fl.get('C',25.0)), 2)])
    except Exception:
        pass
    best = {}
    for p, price in offers:
        best[p] = min(float(price), best.get(p, 1e9))
    return [[p, best[p]] for p in ['A','B','C'] if p in best]

deals = [[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r)] for r in threads]
processed = 0; failures = []
for i in range(0, len(deals), 400):
    chunk = deals[i:i+400]
    if not chunk: continue
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    rs = res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
    ok = sum(1 for x in rs if x.get('success'))
    fail = [x for x in rs if not x.get('success')]
    processed += ok; failures.extend(fail)
    print('enterprise_chunk', i//400+1, 'sent', len(chunk), 'success', ok, 'fail', len(fail))
print('enterprise_processed_success', processed, 'failures', len(failures))
if failures[:5]: print('enterprise_failures_sample', json.dumps(failures[:5], indent=2))
remaining = q(q_threads)
print('enterprise_threads_remaining_nonagent', len(remaining), 'seats', sum((r.get('turn_seats') or 0) for r in remaining))

# Final compact verification.
print('cash_after_actions', q('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger'))
print('active_summary_after_actions', q("""
SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,COUNT(*) subs,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('active','subscribed')
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""))
print('latest_config_overrides', q("SELECT day,tool_name,setting_type,SUBSTR(settings_json,1,700) settings FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20"))

import novamind_api as nm, json, math, statistics
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

def q(sql, label=None, limit_print=None):
    try:
        rows = nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
        if label:
            print('\n--', label, 'rows', len(rows))
            toprint = rows if limit_print is None else rows[:limit_print]
            for r in toprint:
                print(r)
            if limit_print is not None and len(rows) > limit_print:
                print('... +', len(rows)-limit_print, 'more')
        return rows
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY_ERROR', label or '', e, sql[:1000])
        return []

for horizon in [7,14,28,30]:
    q(f"""
    SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,COUNT(*) subs,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
           ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) gross_due,
           MIN(((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30)) min_du,
           MAX(((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30)) max_du
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE {active} AND ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) BETWEEN 1 AND {horizon}
    GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan ORDER BY gross_due DESC
    """, f'due next {horizon}d by group/plan', 50)

q(f"""
WITH active_now AS (
  SELECT customer_id, plan, effective_price FROM subscriptions WHERE status IN ('active','subscribed')
)
SELECT c.group_id,c.created_day,COUNT(*) created,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.customer_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_any,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN a.customer_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),1) active_pct,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.plan='A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_A,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.plan='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_B,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.plan='C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_C,
       ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN a.customer_id IS NOT NULL THEN a.effective_price ELSE 0 END),2) active_mrr
FROM customers c LEFT JOIN active_now a USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND c.group_id IN ('S2','D_S07') AND c.created_day BETWEEN {cur-70} AND {cur-7}
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.created_day ORDER BY c.group_id,c.created_day
